05a040cb8f4d1087b8312af9b83d7b948199adb0:
The TY2025 standard deduction for Married filing jointly is $31,500.  
Form 1040 line 12 is where the standard deduction for Married filing jointly is reported.
3b0ef84cf43e6b7216d62a17f5a8218c687bcff7:
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately start at 10% for income up to $11,925.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 12% for income between $11,925 and $48,475.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 22% for income between $48,475 and $103,350.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 24% for income between $103,350 and $197,300.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 32% for income between $197,300 and $250,525.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 35% for income between $250,525 and $375,800.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married individuals filing separately are 37% for income above $375,800.  
The thresholds for married individuals filing separately match those for single filers except in the top two tax brackets.
3b8d2ab8277cb0168a03a1b96aea9febea185a0d:
TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for married filing jointly start at 10% for income up to $23,850.  
The 12% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $23,850 and $96,950.  
The 22% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $96,950 and $206,700.  
The 24% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $206,700 and $394,600.  
The 32% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $394,600 and $501,050.  
The 35% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income between $501,050 and $751,600.  
The 37% tax bracket for married filing jointly applies to income above $751,600.
46637cad0ce1ef3038c44b75519b6e37ff7a01b3:
The TY2025 standard deduction for Married filing separately is $15,750.  
The amount of $15,750 is reported on Form 1040 line 12.
46d76149175642160cea72f87e55eeb879e88729:
TY2025 ordinary income tax brackets for single filers include a 10% rate for income up to $11,925.  
The 12% tax rate applies to income between $11,925 and $48,475 for single filers.  
Single filers are taxed at a 22% rate on income between $48,475 and $103,350.  
The 24% tax rate for single filers applies to income between $103,350 and $197,300.  
Single filers face a 32% tax rate on income between $197,300 and $250,525.  
The 35% tax rate for single filers applies to income between $250,525 and $626,350.  
Single filers are taxed at a 37% rate on income above $626,350.
50f94cc553cebfb6dacd0d7b2213fbd0a0105c39: A taxpayer claims the LARGER of the standard deduction or total itemized deductions on Form 1040 line 12. Most W-2 wage earners take the standard deduction. W-2 wage earners typically find their itemized deductions to be smaller than the standard deduction.
78b2525ad6f94d4dd255f26edfc5910fa24a36e8:
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 10% for income up to $17,000.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 12% for income between $17,000 and $64,850.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 22% for income between $64,850 and $103,350.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 24% for income between $103,350 and $197,300.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 32% for income between $197,300 and $250,500.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 35% for income between $250,500 and $626,350.  
The TY2025 ordinary income tax bracket for Head of household is 37% for income above $626,350.
884ecc58830807ee68681c4edb0e5519e791f062:
Married couples may file jointly (MFJ), combining both spouses' income on one return.  
Married couples may also file separately (MFS) on two returns.  
Filing jointly (MFJ) usually yields a lower combined tax than filing separately (MFS).
9e6614dbd84dfbffb9174f423f04b450b53f4c8e:
The TY2025 standard deduction for Head of Household is $23,625.  
The TY2025 standard deduction for Head of Household is reported on Form 1040 line 12.
acd1cb96d9b8b9d58c09f1c28939c9ac0acff6da: A taxpayer who is age 65 or older receives an additional standard deduction on top of the basic amount. A taxpayer who is blind also receives an additional standard deduction on top of the basic amount. A taxpayer who is both age 65 or older and blind receives the additional standard deduction twice. Most filers who are under 65 and not blind use only the basic standard deduction.
b3d9f37a9fd250acfadcb3df51587b3fa4b4548e:
The standard deduction for Single filers in TY2025 is $15,750.  
The standard deduction for Married filing jointly in TY2025 is $31,500.  
The standard deduction for Married filing separately in TY2025 is $15,750.  
The standard deduction for Head of household in TY2025 is $23,625.  
Taxpayers should enter the standard deduction amount on Form 1040 line 12.
c02b9788309855a32c2c41a91e877b6a9ded49a8:
Head of household applies to an unmarried taxpayer.  
An unmarried taxpayer must have paid more than half the cost of keeping up a home for a qualifying person.  
A qualifying person can be a dependent child.  
The head of household status must be maintained for more than half the year.  
Head of household status provides a larger standard deduction than Single status.  
Head of household status offers wider tax brackets than Single status.
d4c790569c0173af854a3c5b79b441127d0447e4:
The TY2025 standard deduction for a Single filer is $15,750.  
The TY2025 standard deduction amount for a Single filer is found on Form 1040 line 12.
dcc9420864a35c3fb837001176490e37b5a109d8: Federal income tax brackets are marginal. Each tax rate applies only to the portion of taxable income that falls within that bracket's range. The total tax is calculated by summing the tax from each bracket.
f091343665e4d4ed1b11c01537d4647b4ebdf830:
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is unmarried on the last day of the tax year.  
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is divorced on the last day of the tax year.  
Single filing status applies to a taxpayer who is legally separated on the last day of the tax year.  
A taxpayer with single filing status does not qualify for head of household.
